project.title: Secretome of hTERT-Immortalized Human Mesenchymal Stem Cells
project.projectDescription: The secretome of mesenchymal stromal cells (MSCs) can efficiently stimulate regener-ation and therefore is a tempting remedy for “cell-free cellular therapy”. However, the usage of primary MSC cultures as secretome-producers for translation studies has ob-vious obstacles, including rapid aging of MSC cultures, the need for a large number of verified donors and donor-to-donor variability of secretome content. MSCs immortal-ization allows to overcome those limitations and to obtain secretome-producing cul-tures with prolonged lifetime. However, the efficacy and safety of such secretomes are critical issues, which limit their usage as therapeutic agents. In this study we have tested in large detail how the immortalization of MSC cultures affects the content, bi-ological activity and safety of their secretome. MSCs immortalization via overexpres-sion of human TERT gene does not significantly alter the qualitative and quantitative composition of their secretome or its activity according to the results of proteomic analysis, ELISA, qPCR and functional tests in vitro. Moreover, we have demonstrated that the secretome of immortalized MSCs does not contain detectable amounts of te-lomerase and does not possess any transforming activity. Altogether, our data suggest that immortalized MSC cultures may become a reliable source for obtaining standard-ized active secretome in large-scale quantities for clinical use.The secretome of mesenchymal stromal cells (MSCs) can efficiently stimulate regener-ation and therefore is a tempting remedy for “cell-free cellular therapy”. However, the usage of primary MSC cultures as secretome-producers for translation studies has ob-vious obstacles, including rapid aging of MSC cultures, the need for a large number of verified donors and donor-to-donor variability of secretome content. MSCs immortal-ization allows to overcome those limitations and to obtain secretome-producing cul-tures with prolonged lifetime. However, the efficacy and safety of such secretomes are critical issues, which limit their usage as therapeutic agents. In this study we have tested in large detail how the immortalization of MSC cultures affects the content, bi-ological activity and safety of their secretome. MSCs immortalization via overexpres-sion of human TERT gene does not significantly alter the qualitative and quantitative composition of their secretome or its activity according to the results of proteomic analysis, ELISA, qPCR and functional tests in vitro. Moreover, we have demonstrated that the secretome of immortalized MSCs does not contain detectable amounts of te-lomerase and does not possess any transforming activity. Altogether, our data suggest that immortalized MSC cultures may become a reliable source for obtaining standard-ized active secretome in large-scale quantities for clinical use.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: For proteomic analysis, iMSCs were cultured until 80% confluency was reached (five to seven 100 mm cell culture dishes per sample), then deprived for 16 hours in serum-free DMEM Low Glucose without phenol red (Gibco, #11054020). This was followed by conditioning in the similar medium for 24 hours. The conditioned medium was cleared from cellular debris by centrifugation (2000g, 10’, 4°C) and concentrated 100-fold using MWCO 10 kDa cartridges. Protein concentration in secretome fractions was measured by SDS-PAGE followed with Coomassie Blue Silver G-250 staining and densitometric analysis. Secretome concentrate containing 10-30 μg/μL of total protein was diluted 100-fold by 50 mM bicarbonate-ammonium buffer, pH 8.5, supplemented with 0.05% RapiGest reagent (Waters, #186001861). Then it was sequentially incubated with 2 mM tris-carboxy-methylphosphine (Sigma-Aldrich, #C4706-10G) (one hour at 60 ⁰C), with 4 mM methylmethanethiosulfonate (Sigma-Aldrich, #64306) (15 minutes, room temperature) and with 4 ng/μL trypsin (PanEco, #9002-07-7) (16 hours at 37 ⁰C). Afterwards, formic acid (Applichem, #131030) was added to the samples to the final concentration of 0.1% and the resulting peptides were purified using HLB Oasis columns (Waters, #WAT106202) with following conditions: sorbent equilibration and washing buffer – 0,2% formic acid, elution buffer – 80% acetonitrile with 0,2% formic acid. Chromato-mass spectrometry analysis of the peptides was performed using UltiMate 3000 RSLCnano UHPLC (ThermoFisher Scientific, USA) and timsTOF Pro (Bruker, USA) and QExactive Plus mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw mass spectrometry data were processed using MaxQuant software (version 1.5.8.3) with default parameters. Protein identifications were accepted only if based on at least two unique peptides, with a minimum peptide length of six amino acids. The MS/MS spectra were searched against the Homo sapiens reference proteome database retrieved from UniProt (https://www.uniprot.org/). False discovery rate (FDR) thresholds were set to 1% at both the peptide and protein levels to ensure high-confidence identifications.  The output files generated by MaxQuant, including protein and peptide identification tables, were further processed and explored in Perseus software (version 1.6.2.2). At this stage, data curation involved the removal of contaminant entries and reverse sequences, followed by quality filtering and annotation. Functional information, including Gene Ontology (GO) categories and pathway annotations, was integrated to enable qualitative characterization of the identified proteome.

references.referenceLine: Karagyaur M, Primak A, Basalova N, Monakova A, Tolstoluzhinskaya A, Kulebyakina M, Chechekhina E, Skryabina M, Grigorieva O, Chechekhin V, Yakovleva T, Turilova V, Shagimardanova E, Gazizova G, Vigovskiy M, Kulebyakin K, Sysoeva V, Dyachkova U, Dzhauari S, Bozov K, Popov V, Akopyan Z, Efimenko A, Kalinina N, Tkachuk V. Safety and Regenerative Properties of Immortalized Human Mesenchymal Stromal Cell Secretome. Int J Mol Sci. 2025 26(19):9322

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Characteristics[Organism]: primary isolated human mesenchymal stromal cells (MSCs)
Characteristics[MaterialType]: The secretome of human MSC cultures was obtained as it was described previously.
Characteristics[GeneticModification]: MSCs immortalization via the overexpression of human TERT gene
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: with 4 mM methylmethanethiosulfonate (Sigma-Aldrich, #64306) (15 min, room temperature)
Comment[Instrument]: UltiMate 3000 RSLCnano UHPLC (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA)
Comment[Separation]: Chromatography-mass spectrometry analysis of the peptides was performed using the UltiMate 3000 RSLCnano UHPLC (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA) and a timsTOF Pro (Bruker, Billerica, MA, USA).
